When do trappings of authority matter most in determining others' views of you? They're most potent when people meet you for the first time and you're making an initial impression. It's during these times when others don't know much about you that they will be likely to make judgments about you based on limited information. That's when they'll look for and use immediately observable cues of authority, such as how you dress and speak. So when dealing with someone for the first time, it's especially important to dress at a level that matches your true credentials and to speak with the conviction you truly feel about your offer.
